title: festuca californica subsp. parishii
intro: Festuca californica subsp. parishii, also known as Parish's fescue, is a subspecies of grass native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Parish's fescue include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and potentially treating digestive issues due to its antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and infusing the leaves in hot water to make a tea that can be consumed for its medicinal benefits.
